title: Can't pass an unpacked array as a parameter to module
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature? 

```systemverilog
module bug #(
    parameter N = 1,
    parameter logic signed [8-1:0] ARRAY[N] = '{default: 0}
) (
    input                       clk,
    input  signed       [8-1:0] data_i,
    output logic signed [8-1:0] data_o[N]
);

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) begin
      data_o[N] <= data_i + ARRAY[i];
    end
  end

endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

Output when trying to set `ARRAY` with a `-G` flag

```bash
export VERILATOR_ROOT='/usr/local/share/verilator'
Starting Verilator 5.046 2026-02-28 rev v5.046-55-g1264184fb
- Verilator.cpp:673:  Option --verilate: Start Verilation
- V3File.cpp:245:        --check-times failed: different command line
- V3ParseImp.cpp:301: parseFile: verilated_std_waiver
- V3PreShell.cpp:90:  Preprocessing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std_waiver.vlt
- V3PreShell.cpp:143:     Reading /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std_waiver.vlt
- V3ParseImp.cpp:417: Lexing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std_waiver.vlt
- V3ParseImp.cpp:301: parseFile: verilated_std
- V3PreShell.cpp:90:  Preprocessing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3PreShell.cpp:143:     Reading /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3ParseImp.cpp:417: Lexing /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv
- V3ParseImp.cpp:301: parseFile: bug
- V3PreShell.cpp:90:  Preprocessing bug.sv
- V3PreShell.cpp:143:     Reading bug.sv
- V3ParseImp.cpp:373: Writing all preprocessed output to obj_dir/Vbug__inputs.vpp
- V3ParseImp.cpp:417: Lexing bug.sv
- V3Global.cpp:157:   Removing unused std:: package
- V3LinkLevel.cpp:42: modSortByLevel()
- V3Ast.cpp:1398:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbug_002_cellsort.tree
- V3LinkDot.cpp:5995: linkDotPrimary:
%Error: bug.sv:3:36: Illegal base character: {
-node: CONST 0x5555573a4e10 <e1850#> {e3bk} @dt=0@  ?44?h0
    3 |     parameter logic signed [8-1:0] ARRAY[N] = '{default: 0}
      |                                    ^~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.046 for more assistance.
- V3Ast.cpp:1398:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbug_005_linkdot-preerr.tree
- V3LinkJump.cpp:483: linkJump:
- V3Ast.cpp:1398:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbug_009_linkjump.tree
- V3LinkInc.cpp:364:  linkIncrements:
- V3Ast.cpp:1398:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbug_010_linkinc.tree
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
- V3Ast.cpp:1398:     Dumping obj_dir/Vbug_990_final.tree
- V3StatsReport.cpp:233:statsReport:
%Error: Aborting since under --debug
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=/usr/local/share/verilator
%Error: ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec setarch --addr-no-randomize /usr/local/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --binary -j 0 bug.sv -GN=2 -GARRAY=\'\{8\'h0\,\ 8\'h0\} --debug
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec setarch --addr-no-randomize /usr/local/bin/verilator_bin_dbg --binary -j 0 bug.sv -GN=2 -GARRAY=\'\{8\'h0\,\ 8\'h0\} --debug
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```bash
verilator --binary -j 0 bug.sv -GN=2 -GARRAY="'{8'h0, 8'h0}" --debug
```

**Note:** this works if I only pass `-GN=2`. I have tried all kinds of variations / escape characters for the `ARRAY` parameter

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

5.0.46

What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 22.04

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Sure!

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
